summary:
• The Strait of Hormuz blockade is creating acute supply disruption risks with Brent crude rallying toward $120/barrel and fertilizer prices doubling, while alternative routes like the Panama Canal are absorbing redirected tanker traffic.

• Middle East geopolitical escalation—including the Iran conflict destroying 1.6 million bpd of oil demand and Trump's conditioning of sanctions relief on nuclear negotiations—is driving sustained price volatility and months of expected market disruption.

• Russian oil supply resilience remains intact despite ongoing drone strikes on production facilities, with OPEC+ cohesion holding as Moscow commits to the alliance, limiting upside relief from alternative producers like Venezuela and US LNG exports.
